main_1.label: Battle Basics
main_1.title: How QuizMon Battles Work
pages: In a battle, answering IS attacking. Pick a move, then answer the quiz question that appears.
A correct answer lands your attack. A wrong answer, or letting the timer run out, makes the attack miss.
Answer FAST for a speed bonus, and chain correct answers to build a streak - both make your hits stronger.
Tip: if a fight is going badly you can FLEE from the menu. Retreating almost always works now.

main_1.label: Science & Geography
main_1.title: Study Hall: Science & Geography
pages: SCIENCE: Water is made of hydrogen and oxygen (H2O). Plants make food from sunlight through photosynthesis.
SCIENCE: The Sun is a star. Earth is the third planet from the Sun, and the Moon orbits the Earth.
GEOGRAPHY: The seven continents are Africa, Antarctica, Asia, Europe, North America, Oceania and South America.
GEOGRAPHY: Mount Everest is the tallest mountain, and the Nile and Amazon are among the longest rivers.

main_1.label: Math & Vocabulary
main_1.title: Study Hall: Math & Vocabulary
pages: MATH: Remember order of operations - do brackets and multiplication/division before addition/subtraction.
MATH: A prime number has exactly two factors: 1 and itself (2, 3, 5, 7, 11...). Practice your times tables!
VOCABULARY: Synonyms mean the same thing (big / large); antonyms mean the opposite (hot / cold).
VOCABULARY: Learn common prefixes - 're-' means again, 'un-' means not, 'pre-' means before.

main_1.label: History
main_1.title: Study Hall: History
pages: HISTORY: Events are ordered on a timeline. 'BCE' counts down toward year 1; 'CE' counts up after it.
HISTORY: Ancient civilisations like Egypt, Greece and Rome shaped writing, government and science.
HISTORY: When you read a question, look for date clues and famous names to narrow down the answer.
